blocks.text: Wound care referral for packing changes
Diverticulitis of large intestine with perforation and abscess without bleeding
Ostomy productive, tolerating diet. Midportion of wound has opened up.
Soft abdomen, ostomy productive and pink, small ~3cm open of the midline wound midportion and upon probing
extends some inferiorly and to the level of abdominal wall which feels intact. No significant purulence.
s/p robotic converted to open LAR, end colostomy
Diverticulitis of large Intestine with perforation and abscess without bleeding
Some staples removed, but left the majority
Wound care referral for packing changes
E-signed by Victor Yoon on 03/17/2026 12:16PM PDT
This page was generated at 03/17/2026 12:16PM PDT
colon was sharply excised, and passed off the table with the specimen. The colostomy was then matured in Brooke fashion using
sutures of 3-0 Vicryl. Ostomy appliance was placed.
This concluded the procedure. All counts were correct. I was present for the entire procedure.
Electronically Signed By: Yoon, Victor DO
Perform by Yoon DO, Victor on February 20, 2026 13:34 PST

There was a large subcostal incision in the left upper quadrant, and decision was made to enter the abdomen in the right upper
quadrant. Incision was made in the right upper quadrant to accommodate a robotic trocar. Veress needle was inserted and the
abdomen was then insufflated to a pressure of 15 mmHg. The patient tolerated insufflation well. Veress needle was removed
and a robotic trocar was inserted. Robotic camera was inserted and the abdomen was inspected. There were copious abdominal
adhesions, there were colonic and omental adhesions to the midline, as well as the incisional hernia noted, I tried to maneuver
around the incisions, I noticed that the sigmoid was very densely adherent to its surroundings, decision was made to perform an
open procedure. Robotic camera was removed.
Midline incision was made from the epigastric-area to the suprapubic area. Subcutaneous tissues were divided using
electrocautery, and the midportion of the abdomen, incisional hernia was encountered, and hernia sac was dissected free from its
subcutaneous attachments down to the level of fascia. Hernia sac was sharply incised, the contents were reduced, I was then
able to incise the fascia and this was achieved by dissecting thickened omental adhesions as well as colonic adhesions from the
midline, and then incising fascia in a serial manner. Once the midline was cleared and the fascia had been opened, I was then
able to dissect omental as well as small bowel adhesions to the anterior abdominal wall and bring these down. The omentum
was then reflected cephalad, Bookwalter retractor was placed. Small bowel was densely adherent to the pelvis, and small bowel
adhesions were sharply lysed. There were 2 areas of inadvertent serosal injury, secondary to very dense adhesions, and these
were repaired using sutures of 3-0 silk in Lembert fashion.
Small bowel was then retracted cephalad. The left colon was then mobilized along the line of Toldt, colonic mesentery and
retroperitoneum were separated along the avascular plane, the sigmoid was noted to be very inflamed, thickened. I mobilized
the sigmoid away from the pelvis by using sharp dissection, as it was very densely adherent to this area. The proximal rectum
and distal sigmoid were plastered to the pelvis, anterior pelvis, and I needed to perform finger fracture in order to separate the
colon from these areas. Once I was able to mobilize the sigmoid, the proximal rectum was also extremely adherent to its
surroundings, and I had to further finger fracture this area away from its pelvic attachments. Once this was completed, I was
able to reach the soft portion of the rectum, which was in the midportion. A window was created in the mesentery of
the descending colon, and the colon divided using a contour stapler and a green load. The mesentery was then taken down to
the proximal rectum using Ligasure, some of the mesorectum was divided in order for us to get down to the mid rectum for
stapler. Contour stapler was then used to divide the mid rectum, using a green load. The low anterior resection specimen was
then passed off the table. Bleeding from the sigmoid mesentery was oversewn using 2-0 silk in figure-of-eight fashion.
Hemostasis was satisfactory. The rectal stump was tagged with a 2-0 Prolene.
The left colon was then mobilized further along the white line of Toldt, separating mesentery from retroperitoneum along the
avascular plane, up to the level of the splenic flexure. There is significant scarring in the area secondary to the patient's prior left
nephrectomy. The splenic flexure was mobilized from attachments using sharp and blunt dissection. The gastrocolic ligament
was opened, and this allowed me to fully mobilize the splenic flexure. Hemostasis was satisfactory.
Circular left lower quadrant incision was then made for ostomy site. A column was seen and subcutaneous tissue was sharply
excised and discarded. The fascia was incised in a cruciate manner, the rectus muscle was split and the remainder of the
abdominal wall was incised using electrocautery. The colon was then brought through the ostomy site, care was taken to avoid
twisting. No tension was noted on the colostomy.
Bilateral myocutaneous flaps were then raised so that there could be at tension-free closure. This was done by incising the
subcutaneous tissue away from the anterior fascia in all directions. The abdomen was then copiously irrigated and free fluid was
suctioned. Hemostasis was satisfactory. 19 French channel drain was placed in the pelvis and left paracolic gutter and exited
out a right lower quadrant stab incision. The drain was secured using nylon.
There was still some hernia sac present and this was sharply excised free down to the level of the fascia and discarded. The
abdomen was then closed using 2 sutures of 1 looped PDS in running fashion. About every 3 to 4 cm a widely spaced suture of 1
Vicryl in interrupted fashion was placed in order to serve as internal retention sutures, thereby completing our complex
closure, and also completing primary repair of the patient's incisional hernia. 4000 mg of Restrata Minimatrix was placed into the
subcutaneous space in order to mitigate infection and promote wound healing. Penrose drain was placed at the base of the
wound and exited out the inferior portion of the incision. The skin was closed using skin staples. The Penrose drain was
secured to the skin using a stitch of Vicryl.
Skin staples were used to close the right upper quadrant incision. Dry clean dressings were placed.
Next we turned our attention to maturing the ostomy. The colon was very thickened at the ostomy, a couple centimeters of

80F with prior history of left nephrectomy and cholecystectomy, presented after recent admission with recurrent diverticulitis. She
had intractable pain and failure of medical management and she was taken for OR.
ROBOTIC ASSITED DIAGNOSTIC LAPAROSCOPY, CONVERTED TO OPEN LOW ANTERIOR RESECTION, EXTENSIVE LYSIS OF
ADHESIONS 40MINUTES, REPAIR OF SMALL BOWEL SEROSAL INJURY X 2, MOBILIZATION OF SPLENIC FLEXURE, END
COLOSTOMY, BILATERAL MYOCUTANEOUS FLAPS, PRIMARY REPAIR OF INCISIONAL HERNIA 5CM DEFECT, COMPLEX CLOSURE
WITH INTERNAL RETENTION SUTURES, RESTRATA 4000MG TO SUBCUTANEOUS SPACE
Copious intra-abdominal adhesions, extremely dense adhesions of sigmoid and proximal rectum to the pelvis, inflamed and
The patient was brought to the operating room and laid supine on operating table. General anesthesia was administered. Foley
catheter was placed and arms were tucked. Zosyn IV was administered for surgical site prophylaxis. SCDs were utilized for DVT
prophylaxis. The patient was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion. A timeout was performed verifying correct patient,
procedure, site, position, equipment prior to beginning.
FINAL SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT for ONEIL, CHARLOTTE A (SAC26-4648)
A Sonic Healthcare Anatomic Pathology Proctice
Received: 02/20/2026 Time: 12:00pm Physician: VICTOR YOON, D.O.
DIVERTICULITIS WITH MUCOSAL EROSION, SUBMUCOSAL ABSCESS, AND PERFORATION.
Microscopic examination performed and the results incorporated into the final diagnosis. All controls stained appropriately.
The specimen is received in formalin, labeled with the proper patient identification and "low anterior colon section" consists of 19.3 X
3.4 cm in diameter segment of colon with surrounding pericolonic adipose tissue. The serosal surfaces are gray-tan to brown, diffusely
congested with adhesions and there are three, adjacent 0.7 to 2.7 cm transmural defects, which is 3.8 cm to the nearest resection
margin. The specimen is sectioned revealing a gray-tan, redundantly folded mucosa with diffuse diverticula and a thickened colon wall
with two possible areas of perforation, which are inked black. Additionally found within the container is a 2.4 cm in length X 2.2 cm in
diameter additional segment of colon which has a gray-pink and glistening mucosa. The specimen is representatively sampled as follows:
Technical Component was performed at LMC PATHOLOGY SERVICES 7455W. WASHINGTON AVE, STE 301, LAS VEGAS, NV 89128 CLIA #29D0976712, CAP #7117101 JONATHAN H HUGHES M.D. Ph.D MEDICAL DIRECTOR,
